Windom is the county seat. Lake Shetek State Park is southwestern Minnesota's largest lake and recreational area. GRE (Great River Energy) and Minnesota Power's wind transmission infrastructure crosses Cottonwood County, connecting wind generation to the MISO (Midcontinent Independent System Operator) grid. The county's agricultural economy and wind energy transition create a distinctive combination of precision agriculture IT and renewable energy grid management.

restrictions.name: Grimes County
restrictions.title: Grimes County TX — Anderson/Navasota/east Texas Brazos River heritage/Sam Houston proximity
restrictions.description: Grimes County straddles the Brazos River between Houston and Bryan-College Station — two of Texas's largest metro areas — creating a county that sits within fiber backbone reach of both markets. Navasota is the commercial center, while Anderson is the county seat. The county has strong connections to Texas Republic-era history, and Sam Houston's Huntsville is in adjacent Walker County. Grimes County's position between Houston's petrochemical complex and Texas A&M University's agricultural research network creates demand for both industrial and academic technology services. Lick Creek Park and the Brazos River corridor also generate recreation and conservation management IT.

restrictions.name: Hill County
restrictions.title: Hill County TX — Hillsboro/I-35 central Texas corridor/Corsicana adjacent
restrictions.description: Hill County sits directly on I-35 between Dallas-Fort Worth and Waco — one of the highest-traffic freight corridors in the United States. Hillsboro is the county seat, and the I-35/I-35E/I-35W split occurs just north of the county in the DFW metro. The county's position in the I-35 logistics corridor creates fleet management, freight logistics, and truck stop technology infrastructure. Hill County is part of the North Texas/Central Texas data center growth region, with Waco's expanding data center market (driven by cheap power and I-35 connectivity) beginning to influence Hill County's development prospects. Oncor Electric's transmission infrastructure crosses the county as part of the ERCOT grid.

restrictions.name: Scott County
restrictions.title: Scott County VA — Gate City/Daniel Boone Wilderness Trail/SW Virginia coalfields
restrictions.description: Scott County in far southwest Virginia sits along the Daniel Boone Wilderness Trail — the historic route through the Cumberland Gap that brought settlers into Kentucky and Tennessee. Gate City is the county seat. The county is one of Virginia's most remote — bordered by Tennessee and Kentucky, it is further from Richmond than most Virginia counties get. Scott County's coal mining heritage connects it to the broader Central Appalachian energy economy, and its participation in the Coalfields Expressway project (connecting SW Virginia to I-81) reflects the ongoing infrastructure investment in Virginia's most isolated region. ARC and federal Appalachian broadband programs have specifically targeted Scott County for connectivity expansion.

restrictions.name: Smyth County
restrictions.title: Smyth County VA — Marion/Holston River/Mount Rogers/SW Virginia Appalachian
restrictions.description: Smyth County in southwest Virginia is centered on Marion, a small city on the Holston River along I-81. Mount Rogers — Virginia's highest peak at 5,729 feet — rises in the adjacent Grayson County, but the Mount Rogers National Recreation Area's southern approaches extend into Smyth County. The Holston River here is managed by TVA, connecting Smyth County to the Tennessee Valley Authority's water and power management computing network. Smyth County's position on I-81 (one of Appalachia's primary transportation spines) and the region's emerging outdoor recreation economy have driven broadband investment in the New River Valley's westernmost counties.

restrictions.name: Hardin County
restrictions.title: Hardin County IL — Elizabethtown/smallest IL county/Ohio River/Shawnee National Forest
restrictions.description: Hardin County is Illinois's smallest county by population — a remote Ohio River county at the southern tip of the state, surrounded by the Shawnee National Forest on three sides and Kentucky across the river. Elizabethtown (not to be confused with Elizabethtown KY) is the county seat, reachable only by state highways through the Shawnee forest. The Garden of the Gods wilderness area in the Shawnee National Forest is within Hardin County. Cave-In-Rock State Park, where Ohio River pirates once operated a tavern, attracts heritage tourism. Hardin County's extreme remoteness makes it a meaningful case study for Illinois's last-mile broadband challenges — it appears regularly in state rural broadband gap analysis.

restrictions.name: Alexander County
restrictions.title: Alexander County IL — Cairo/confluence of Mississippi and Ohio/southernmost IL
restrictions.description: Alexander County contains Cairo (pronounced KAY-ro), Illinois — the southernmost city in Illinois and one of the most historically significant river cities in America. Cairo sits at the precise confluence of the Mississippi and Ohio rivers — the strategic location that made it a vital Union Army supply base during the Civil War and later one of the busiest river ports in the inland US. The county's dramatic population decline (from 21,000 in 1900 to under 7,000 today) reflects deindustrialization, but its geographic position at the confluence of the nation's two great rivers — and the US Army Corps of Engineers' extensive flood control infrastructure — creates federal administrative IT that remains significant relative to the county's small population.

restrictions.name: Wayne County
restrictions.title: Wayne County MO — Greenville/Current River/SE Missouri Ozarks/Clearwater Lake
restrictions.description: Wayne County in southeast Missouri occupies the Current River's upper watershed in the Ozark Highlands — one of the most remote counties in Missouri. Greenville is the county seat. Clearwater Lake, impounded by a US Army Corps of Engineers dam, provides flood control and recreation infrastructure with federal water management IT. The Ozark National Scenic Riverways — America's first national scenic river, designated in 1964 — traverses Wayne County, with National Park Service IT infrastructure for river management and visitor services. Wayne County's extreme rural character and national park presence create a counterintuitive technology demand profile for a county of 13,000 people.

restrictions.name: Stone County
restrictions.title: Stone County MO — Galena/Table Rock Lake/Branson adjacent/Silver Dollar City corridor
restrictions.description: Stone County borders Taney County — home of Branson, Missouri's country music and family entertainment capital — and shares Table Rock Lake, one of the most popular recreation lakes in the Ozarks. Galena is the county seat. The Table Rock Lake area draws over 7 million visitors annually to both Stone and Taney counties combined, generating massive hospitality IT, resort management systems, and visitor logistics computing. Silver Dollar City (in adjacent Stone County territory near the Taney line) is a major Ozark theme park. Stone County's position in Missouri's fastest-growing tourism region and proximity to Branson's entertainment infrastructure creates strong connectivity demand.

restrictions.name: Phelps County
restrictions.title: Phelps County NE — Holdrege/Republican River/Nebraska irrigation district hub
restrictions.description: Phelps County in south-central Nebraska centers on Holdrege — one of the primary hubs of Nebraska's Republican River irrigation district. The county is part of the Central Nebraska Public Power and Irrigation District (CNPPID) service area, where an extensive system of canals, reservoirs, and pumping stations manages water for hundreds of thousands of irrigated acres. CNPPID's SCADA and water management computing infrastructure is among the most complex agricultural water control systems in the Great Plains. Holdrege's position as a regional agricultural service center and the irrigation district's operational complexity create institutional IT infrastructure well above what a county of 9,000 people would typically support.

restrictions.name: Richardson County
restrictions.title: Richardson County NE — Falls City/Nebraska-Kansas-Missouri tri-state corner/Big Nemaha River
restrictions.description: Richardson County occupies Nebraska's southeastern corner, where Nebraska, Kansas, and Missouri converge along the Missouri River's tributary valleys. Falls City is the county seat, named for the falls of the Nemaha River. The county has an unusual geographic position — it borders both Kansas and Missouri within a few miles, creating a multi-state commerce and logistics zone. Richardson County's Big Nemaha Natural Resources District manages watershed IT for flood control and water quality monitoring. The county's agricultural economy (corn, soybeans, cattle) and proximity to the greater Kansas City market create a connectivity corridor through southeast Nebraska that includes fiber routes along major highways.

restrictions.name: Armstrong County
restrictions.title: Armstrong County TX — Claude/Palo Duro Canyon adjacent/Texas Panhandle caprock
restrictions.description: Armstrong County in the Texas Panhandle borders Palo Duro Canyon State Park — the 'Grand Canyon of Texas' — and its county seat is the small town of Claude. The county sits on the Llano Estacado (Staked Plains) caprock escarpment, where the high plains drop dramatically into the Red River drainage system. Armstrong County is extremely rural — one of the least populated counties in Texas — but its position near Amarillo (30 miles north) places it within reach of the Amarillo metro's fiber infrastructure. The Panhandle's wind energy development includes transmission lines crossing Armstrong County as part of the CREZ (Competitive Renewable Energy Zone) build-out connecting Panhandle wind to Texas population centers.

restrictions.name: Duval County
restrictions.title: Duval County TX — San Diego/south Texas brush country/Eagle Ford shale/George Parr machine
restrictions.description: Duval County in south Texas is best known historically as the domain of George Parr — the 'Duke of Duval' — whose political machine delivered votes in the contested 1948 US Senate race that launched Lyndon Johnson's national career. San Diego (Texas) is the county seat. Duval County sits in the Eagle Ford Shale play — south Texas's primary oil and natural gas production formation — with significant upstream oil field IT: well monitoring, production data systems, and pipeline SCADA infrastructure. The county's brush country terrain and proximity to the Laredo energy corridor create consistent demand for remote monitoring and energy production IT.

restrictions.name: Starr County
restrictions.title: Starr County TX — Rio Grande City/US-Mexico border/Roma historic district/Border Patrol
restrictions.description: Starr County on the Rio Grande is one of Texas's poorest counties by income but one of the most significant for federal border operations. Rio Grande City is a crossing point for both commerce and immigration, with CBP (Customs and Border Protection) processing infrastructure and Border Patrol sector IT. Roma, a National Historic Landmark district in Starr County, preserves some of the best-surviving Rio Grande border town architecture in Texas. The Roma Lock Dam on the Rio Grande is an international water-sharing structure managed jointly with Mexico under the International Boundary and Water Commission (IBWC). Roma's historic designation and IBWC infrastructure create federal computing investment in a county better known for its challenging economic indicators.

restrictions.name: Adams County
restrictions.title: Adams County WI — Friendship/Wisconsin Dells adjacent/central Wisconsin sand country
restrictions.description: Adams County in central Wisconsin borders Juneau County (Wisconsin Dells) and sits in Wisconsin's central sand plains — a glacially-outwashed sandstone region that has become one of the world's leading sources of frac sand for the oil and gas industry. The frac sand mining boom brought industrial mining IT (conveyor automation, processing plant SCADA, logistics management) to a previously agricultural county. Adams County's position adjacent to Wisconsin Dells — Wisconsin's largest resort destination — also generates hospitality and tourism technology demand. Consolidated Communications (formerly TDS) has built fiber infrastructure in the Dells corridor that extends into Adams County.

restrictions.name: Ashland County
restrictions.title: Ashland County WI — Ashland/Lake Superior/Chequamegon Bay/Bad River Band
restrictions.description: Ashland County sits on the south shore of Lake Superior's Chequamegon Bay — one of the most protected deep-water harbors on the lake. Ashland was a significant Lake Superior iron ore and lumber shipping port in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. The Bad River Band of the Lake Superior Tribe of Chippewa Indians governs territory within Ashland County and has been central to the controversy over the proposed Back Forty iron ore mine in adjacent Menominee County. The Apostle Islands National Lakeshore headquarters is in Bayfield (adjacent Bayfield County), but Ashland serves as the regional commercial hub, with NPS administrative IT extending across the area. Northland College in Ashland provides small college technology infrastructure.

restrictions.name: Green County
restrictions.title: Green County WI — Monroe/Swiss cheese heritage/New Glarus/Swiss settlement
restrictions.description: Green County in south-central Wisconsin is the Swiss cheese capital of America — Monroe is home to some of the largest Swiss and Limburger cheese producers in the United States. The town of New Glarus was settled in 1845 by Swiss immigrants and maintains an exceptionally well-preserved Swiss alpine heritage. Green County's dairy industry generates food processing IT — milk quality monitoring, cold chain management, and agricultural production tracking. New Glarus Brewing Company has become one of the most sought-after craft breweries in the Midwest (distribution limited to Wisconsin only). The county's proximity to Madison (30 miles north) places it within the University of Wisconsin research network's influence.

restrictions.name: Kemper County
restrictions.title: Kemper County MS — De Kalb/Kemper County Energy Facility/east Mississippi
restrictions.description: Kemper County became nationally prominent as the site of the Kemper County Energy Facility — Mississippi Power's attempt to build the first commercial-scale coal gasification power plant in the United States. The project, often called 'Mississippi's Billion Dollar Blunder,' ballooned from $2.9 billion to over $7.5 billion before being converted to natural gas in 2017. Despite the project's failure, the facility left behind substantial electrical and industrial IT infrastructure in a rural east Mississippi county. De Kalb is the county seat. Kemper County's position in the Alabama-Mississippi energy corridor and the CLECO/Southern Company transmission network gives it grid connections that exceed what a county of 10,000 people would otherwise have.

restrictions.name: Clay County
restrictions.title: Clay County MS — West Point/automotive manufacturing/Golden Triangle corridor
restrictions.description: Clay County anchors the west end of Mississippi's Golden Triangle manufacturing corridor — the Columbus-West Point-Starkville manufacturing and research region that has attracted significant industrial investment. West Point is home to automotive and manufacturing operations, and the county's position near the Toyota plant in Blue Springs (Union County) and the Mississippi State University-anchored Starkville technology corridor creates industrial supply chain IT and precision manufacturing computing. The Tennessee-Tombigbee Waterway runs through Clay County, providing barge access that has attracted industrial development. Columbus Air Force Base (in adjacent Lowndes County) contributes to the regional technology base.

restrictions.name: Hempstead County
restrictions.title: Hempstead County AR — Hope/Clinton birthplace/SW Arkansas natural gas/Millwood Lake
restrictions.description: Hempstead County is best known as the birthplace of Bill Clinton (in Hope) — a fact that generated enormous heritage tourism and associated IT development following Clinton's 1992 presidential victory. The Clinton Birthplace Home National Historic Site in Hope is a National Park Service unit with federal administrative IT. Hope is also a significant agricultural market center for southwest Arkansas's watermelon belt (Hope Watermelon Festival) and the adjacent natural gas production area. Millwood Lake, impounded by the US Army Corps of Engineers on the Little River, provides flood control and recreation infrastructure. Southwestern Electric Power Company (SWEPCO) serves the county's industrial and agricultural power needs.

restrictions.name: Monroe County
restrictions.title: Brinkley/White River NWR/White River delta/Arkansas County adjacent
restrictions.description: Monroe County in the Arkansas Delta sits along the White River — one of the last large bottomland hardwood forest systems in the Mississippi Valley. Brinkley is the county seat and a significant agricultural service center. The White River National Wildlife Refuge (partly in Monroe County) is one of the largest bottomland hardwood refuges in the US, managed by the US Fish and Wildlife Service with federal ecological monitoring and hydrological tracking IT. Monroe County is notable for occasional reported sightings of the ivory-billed woodpecker — once considered extinct — in its vast bottomland forests, generating scientific interest and remote monitoring infrastructure. The county's extensive rice and soybean farming operations use precision agriculture technology across wide, flat delta fields.

restrictions.name: Hamblen County
restrictions.title: Hamblen County TN — Morristown/NE Tennessee manufacturing/Nolichucky River corridor
restrictions.description: Hamblen County is a manufacturing hub in northeast Tennessee — Morristown hosts a diverse industrial base including automotive parts suppliers, food processing, and precision manufacturing. Lincoln Electric, Bridgestone, and other major manufacturers have operations in Morristown, creating significant industrial IT. Walters State Community College in Morristown serves the technical workforce training pipeline for northeast Tennessee's manufacturing economy. The Nolichucky River's hydroelectric generation (operated by TVA) provides reliable industrial power. Hamblen County's manufacturing density — exceptional for a county of 64,000 people — reflects northeast Tennessee's position in the Appalachian auto parts supply chain serving plants in Kentucky, Ohio, and South Carolina.

restrictions.name: Lincoln County
restrictions.title: Lincoln County TN — Fayetteville/south Tennessee/Elk River/Davy Crockett birthplace adjacent
restrictions.description: Lincoln County in south-central Tennessee centers on Fayetteville — a small city on the Elk River known for its antebellum courthouse square and regional agricultural market function. The county borders Alabama and is part of the Huntsville (AL) economic influence zone — increasingly, Lincoln County residents commute to Huntsville's aerospace and defense industries, bringing higher broadband expectations than comparable rural Tennessee counties. The Jack Daniel's distillery tourism corridor (in adjacent Moore County) draws visitors through Lincoln County on their way from Nashville and Chattanooga. Lincoln County's agricultural base includes cattle, corn, and hay, with University of Tennessee Extension precision agriculture programming.

restrictions.name: Rice County
restrictions.title: Rice County KS — Lyons/central Kansas/Coronado history/Quivira NWR
restrictions.description: Rice County in central Kansas is notable for Quivira National Wildlife Refuge — one of the most important stopover points for shorebirds and waterfowl along the Central Flyway, where interior salt marshes attract hundreds of thousands of migrating birds. Lyons is the county seat and the location of Coronado Quivira Museum — celebrating Francisco Vásquez de Coronado's 1541 expedition, which reached central Kansas in search of the legendary Seven Cities of Gold. Rice County has significant oil and gas production in the Mid-Continent oil field extension. The Quivira refuge's bird monitoring systems and US Fish and Wildlife Service management IT add federal computing presence to a county otherwise defined by agricultural and energy production.

restrictions.name: Chase County
restrictions.title: Chase County KS — Cottonwood Falls/Tallgrass Prairie National Preserve/Flint Hills
restrictions.description: Chase County contains the Tallgrass Prairie National Preserve — one of the last large intact tallgrass prairie ecosystems in North America, managed jointly by the Nature Conservancy and National Park Service. Cottonwood Falls has what is often cited as the oldest operating courthouse in Kansas — an 1873 limestone Italianate building. William Least Heat-Moon's book 'PrairyErth' (1991) used Chase County as the subject for a deep map of a single American county, making it one of the most literarily examined rural counties in the US. The Flint Hills region's unique geology (too rocky to plow, preserving the original prairie) creates the conditions for both cattle ranching and significant ecological monitoring IT through the Konza Prairie Biological Station (in adjacent Riley County).

restrictions.name: Crawford County
restrictions.title: Crawford County OH — Bucyrus/north-central Ohio/Galion manufacturing/Wyandot heritage
restrictions.description: Crawford County in north-central Ohio centers on Bucyrus — known historically as the 'Bratwurst Capital of America' for its German-American food manufacturing heritage. Galion, the second-largest city, has a history in industrial manufacturing (Galion Iron Works, Galion-Godwin Truck Body Company). Crawford County sits in Ohio's manufacturing heartland, with Tier 2 and Tier 3 auto parts suppliers connected to the GM and Ford assembly plants across northern Ohio. Ohio Central Railroad's heritage and the county's agricultural base (corn, soybeans, hogs) create an agricultural-industrial IT mix. North Central State College in Mansfield (adjacent Richland County) provides community college technical education reaching into Crawford County.

restrictions.name: Defiance County
restrictions.title: Defiance County OH — Defiance/Maumee River/Battle of Fallen Timbers/NW Ohio manufacturing
restrictions.description: Defiance County sits at the confluence of the Maumee and Auglaize rivers in northwest Ohio — near the site of the 1794 Battle of Fallen Timbers, where General Anthony Wayne's victory over a confederation of Native American tribes opened the Northwest Territory to American settlement. Defiance College is a United Church of Christ liberal arts institution founded in 1850. The county's position on the Maumee River corridor (which flows through Toledo to Lake Erie) connects it to northwest Ohio's manufacturing and agricultural processing economy. General Motors has operated a powertrain facility in Defiance — contributing significant automotive manufacturing IT to a county of 38,000 people.

restrictions.name: Appling County
restrictions.title: Appling County GA — Baxley/SE Georgia timber/Altamaha River/Vidalia onion adjacent
restrictions.description: Appling County in southeast Georgia is a timber and agricultural county centered on Baxley and the Altamaha River — one of Georgia's most significant river systems and the largest free-flowing river on the East Coast south of the St. Lawrence. The county sits at the edge of the Vidalia onion growing region (Toombs/Montgomery/Jeff Davis counties) and benefits from the agricultural marketing and logistics infrastructure of the world's most famous sweet onion. Georgia Power's Plant Hatch nuclear generating station (in adjacent Brantley/Appling counties) is one of the Southeast's primary nuclear power plants and a major employer in this area. Plant Hatch's operations generate sophisticated nuclear facility IT including safety systems, regulatory compliance, and process monitoring.

restrictions.name: Long County
restrictions.title: Long County GA — Ludowici/Fort Stewart adjacent/SE Georgia military influence
restrictions.description: Long County in southeast Georgia is dominated by its proximity to Fort Stewart — the US Army's largest installation east of the Mississippi, home to the 3rd Infantry Division. Fort Stewart's base boundary is not in Long County but the county immediately borders it and hosts significant military housing, commercial support, and commuter infrastructure for Stewart's 20,000+ active duty soldiers. Ludowici is the county seat. The military's presence drives Long County's broadband and commercial connectivity investment well above what a county of 19,000 permanent residents would otherwise receive. The Georgia Army National Guard's installations in the region also contribute federal IT infrastructure.

restrictions.name: Concordia Parish
restrictions.title: Concordia Parish LA — Vidalia/Natchez-Vidalia bridge/Mississippi River/James Brown connection
restrictions.description: Concordia Parish sits directly across the Mississippi River from Natchez, Mississippi — connected by the Natchez-Vidalia Bridge. Vidalia is the parish seat. The parish has a remarkable cultural connection: it was the birthplace of soul music pioneer Little Richard (born in Macon GA, raised differently) — actually the namesake Vidalia onion was developed in nearby Georgia, but the bridge-city of Vidalia LA has its own history. The Mississippi River at this point is managed by the US Army Corps of Engineers' Vicksburg District, with river navigation IT and flood control infrastructure. Concordia Parish's energy sector includes natural gas production and the Brame Energy Center (a Louisiana generating station). The Natchez Trace Parkway's northern Louisiana approach passes through the region.

restrictions.name: East Feliciana Parish
restrictions.title: East Feliciana Parish LA — Clinton/Florida Parishes/Audubon Oakley Plantation/rural north Louisiana
restrictions.description: East Feliciana Parish is one of Louisiana's 'Florida Parishes' — the region that was part of Spanish West Florida until the West Florida Rebellion of 1810 created a short-lived republic before US annexation. Clinton is the parish seat, known for its antebellum Greek Revival architecture. The Audubon State Historic Site at Oakley Plantation in adjacent West Feliciana Parish draws visitors to the region where John James Audubon painted many of the birds in 'Birds of America' while working as a tutor in 1821. East Feliciana Parish's rural character and position in the Florida Parishes agricultural corridor has made it a focus of Louisiana's rural broadband expansion programs. The parish's historical legal and civic infrastructure — it has a notably intact 19th-century courthouse and town square — provides institutional continuity unusual for a parish of 20,000 people.

restrictions.name: Rio Blanco County
restrictions.title: Rio Blanco County CO — Meeker/White River oil shale/Rangely oil field/Flat Tops wilderness
restrictions.description: Rio Blanco County in northwest Colorado contains two significant energy production zones: the Rangely Oil Field — one of the oldest and largest oil fields in Colorado — and the vast oil shale deposits of the Piceance Basin. Meeker is the county seat, sitting on the White River where the 1879 Meeker Massacre occurred. The Flat Tops Wilderness Area in the White River National Forest provides backcountry recreation infrastructure managed by the US Forest Service. Rangely's oil field IT — production monitoring, pipeline SCADA, water management — operates continuously across a remote high-desert landscape. Colorado Northwestern Community College in Rangely serves energy sector workforce training for the Piceance Basin.

restrictions.name: Bent County
restrictions.title: Bent County CO — Las Animas/Bent's Fort/Arkansas River/Comanche National Grassland
restrictions.description: Bent County in southeastern Colorado is named for William Bent, whose Bent's Old Fort on the Arkansas River was the most important trading post on the Santa Fe Trail. The reconstructed Bent's Old Fort National Historic Site operates today as a National Park Service unit with federal administrative IT. Las Animas is the county seat, on the Arkansas River near the John Martin Reservoir — a large Army Corps of Engineers impoundment. The Comanche National Grassland to the south is one of the largest national grasslands in the US, with dinosaur trackways, canyon petroglyphs, and US Forest Service management IT. Bent County's position on US-50 (the old Santa Fe Trail route) connects it to Pueblo and beyond.

restrictions.name: Costilla County
restrictions.title: Costilla County CO — San Luis/oldest Colorado town/San Luis Valley/Rio Grande/Sangre de Cristo
restrictions.description: Costilla County contains San Luis — the oldest continuously occupied town in Colorado, founded in 1851. The county sits at the southern end of the San Luis Valley, surrounded by the Sangre de Cristo Mountains to the east. La Sierra (the Commons) is a 77,500-acre community land grant — one of the few surviving Spanish land grants in the US where traditional grazing and gathering rights are legally protected. Great Sand Dunes National Park is in adjacent Alamosa County but its water management systems (the Baca National Wildlife Refuge) extend into Costilla County. The county's Hispanic agricultural heritage, federal land management IT, and rural connectivity challenges make it a significant case study for Colorado's rural broadband equity programs.

restrictions.name: Hinsdale County
restrictions.title: Hinsdale County CO — Lake City/least populous Colorado county/San Juan Mountains/Alferd Packer
restrictions.description: Hinsdale County is Colorado's least populous county — with roughly 800 permanent residents — but it encompasses some of the most dramatic high-altitude terrain in the Rocky Mountains. Lake City, the county seat, sits at 8,671 feet elevation near Slumgullion Earthflow (one of the world's most active landslides). Alferd Packer's 1874 Colorado cannibalism case occurred in Hinsdale County, making it one of America's most notorious frontier crime locations. The San Juan Mountains here exceed 14,000 feet at multiple peaks. Despite its tiny population, Hinsdale County participates in Colorado's BEAD broadband allocations and serves as a gateway for Alpine Loop backcountry recreation, generating USFS management IT and emergency communications infrastructure.

restrictions.name: Lauderdale County
restrictions.title: Lauderdale County AL — Florence/Muscle Shoals adjacent/W.C. Handy birthplace/Wilson Dam TVA
restrictions.description: Lauderdale County contains Florence — the largest city in the Shoals metro area and a center of Alabama's remarkable music heritage. W.C. Handy, the 'Father of the Blues,' was born in Florence in 1873. Lauderdale County borders the Tennessee River at Wilson Dam — the first dam completed by the Tennessee Valley Authority in 1924. Wilson Dam and Wheeler Dam (in adjacent Lawrence County) form the heart of TVA's Tennessee River hydroelectric system, with dam operations IT and reservoir management computing that spans multiple counties. FAME Studios and Muscle Shoals Sound Studio (in adjacent Colbert County, but drawing from the Florence metro) created the 'Muscle Shoals Sound' that defined American soul and rock music from the 1960s through 1980s.

restrictions.name: Blount County
restrictions.title: Blount County AL — Oneonta/Covered Bridge Capital of Alabama/south Appalachian foothills
restrictions.description: Blount County is known as the 'Covered Bridge Capital of Alabama' — it contains more historic covered bridges than any other county in the state, preserved in the foothills of the Appalachian Mountains. Oneonta is the county seat, located in the valley between the Appalachian Plateau and the Piedmont. Blount County has been one of Alabama's fastest-growing exurban counties — residents commute to Birmingham (30 miles south) while living in a rural Appalachian setting. This suburban-rural dynamic has driven significant broadband infrastructure investment, as the commuter population demands metro-level connectivity. Southern Natural Gas pipeline infrastructure crosses Blount County en route to Birmingham.

restrictions.name: Colbert County
restrictions.title: Colbert County AL — Muscle Shoals/FAME Studios/Wilson Dam TVA/Cherokee/Sheffield
restrictions.description: Colbert County is the home of Muscle Shoals — one of the most storied recording locations in American music history. FAME Studios and Muscle Shoals Sound Studio recorded artists including Aretha Franklin, Otis Redding, Wilson Pickett, the Rolling Stones, and Paul Simon. The county seat is Tuscumbia — birthplace of Helen Keller, whose family home Ivy Green is a historic site and pilgrimage destination. Wilson Dam and Pickwick Lake's TVA infrastructure provide significant electrical generating capacity to the county. Colbert County's position in the Tennessee River Valley gives it access to TVA's grid and the industrial power resources that have attracted manufacturing to the Shoals region.

restrictions.name: Cumberland County
restrictions.title: Cumberland County VA — Cumberland Courthouse/Central Virginia/James River watershed
restrictions.description: Cumberland County is a small, rural county in central Virginia between Farmville and Richmond — one of Virginia's less-populous rural counties but situated in a region undergoing broadband expansion as Richmond's metro influence extends west. The James River and Appomattox River watersheds intersect in Cumberland County. The county's timber and agricultural economy is transitioning as central Virginia's data center corridor (anchored in Henrico and adjacent Chesterfield counties near Richmond) expands its geographic reach. Cumberland County's rural character and proximity to I-64 and US-60 give it a developing corridor position for future infrastructure investment.

restrictions.name: Pittsylvania County
restrictions.title: Pittsylvania County VA — Chatham/Danville adjacent/southern VA tobacco/data center growth corridor
restrictions.description: Pittsylvania County is Virginia's largest county by area and surrounds Danville — a city that has transformed from a tobacco manufacturing center into a growing tech and advanced manufacturing hub. The Danville-Pittsylvania area has attracted Microsoft data center investment, and Amazon Web Services has expanded its footprint in the region. Pittsylvania County's vast territory, low land costs, available power infrastructure from Dominion Energy, and position near I-85 (connecting Charlotte to Richmond) create favorable data center conditions. The Southern Virginia Technology Park (in Danville) and the river industrial zones along the Roanoke (Staunton) River provide brownfield redevelopment opportunities for technology facilities.

restrictions.name: Pulaski County
restrictions.title: Pulaski County VA — Pulaski/New River Valley/Radford/Dublin/NRV industrial corridor
restrictions.description: Pulaski County sits in Virginia's New River Valley — one of Appalachia's most industrially active corridors. The county borders Radford (home of Radford University) and Floyd County to the south. The Volvo Trucks manufacturing plant in Dublin (Pulaski County) is one of the largest heavy truck assembly facilities in North America, employing thousands and generating sophisticated automotive manufacturing IT. Radford Army Ammunition Plant, operated by BAE Systems in adjacent Radford city, is one of the US Army's primary propellant production facilities — a significant defense industrial IT installation immediately adjacent to Pulaski County. The New River's unique north-flowing trajectory and its ancient geology make the NRV one of the most geologically distinct valleys in Appalachia.

restrictions.name: Montmorency County
restrictions.title: Montmorency County MI — Atlanta MI/elk herd/AuSable River headwaters/northern LP
restrictions.description: Montmorency County in northern Michigan is home to one of the largest free-ranging elk herds east of the Mississippi — a herd that was reintroduced in the early 20th century and now numbers over 1,000 animals. Atlanta, Michigan (the county seat) is the self-proclaimed 'Elk Capital of Michigan.' The AuSable River, famous for its blue-ribbon fly fishing and the Au Sable River Canoe Marathon, originates in Montmorency County. The county's tourism economy (elk viewing, hunting, fishing, snowmobiling) creates hospitality and outdoor recreation IT. Michigan DNR wildlife management systems for the elk herd — including collar tracking, population monitoring, and hunting permit management — represent unusual wildlife IT for a Michigan county.

restrictions.name: Emmet County
restrictions.title: Emmet County MI — Petoskey/Harbor Springs/Little Traverse Bay/NW Michigan resort
restrictions.description: Emmet County anchors the Little Traverse Bay resort region — one of Michigan's premier vacation destinations. Petoskey is famous for Petoskey stones (fossilized coral) and its Victorian-era Gaslight District, while Harbor Springs has one of the highest per-capita household incomes in Michigan. The Bay Harbor development (former cement plant brownfield redevelopment into a luxury resort and residential community) brought significant hospitality IT to the county. Hemingway's 'Up in Michigan' stories were set in the Horton Bay/Charlevoix area adjacent to Emmet County, and the NW Michigan wine country (Leelanau and Traverse City area) draws visitors through the county. North Central Michigan College in Petoskey serves as a workforce education anchor.

restrictions.name: McHenry County
restrictions.title: McHenry County ND — Towner/Souris River/north-central ND/International Peace Garden adjacent
restrictions.description: McHenry County in north-central North Dakota sits on the Souris (Mouse) River drainage system that flows from Saskatchewan into North Dakota before turning back north into Manitoba. Towner is the county seat. The county is adjacent to the International Peace Garden (in Bottineau County to the west) and shares the rolling glacial prairie landscape of the Missouri Coteau. McHenry County's wheat and sunflower farming creates agricultural cooperative IT infrastructure, and Basin Electric Power Cooperative's grid management coordinates power from lignite plants and wind farms across north-central North Dakota. Rural broadband priority areas include McHenry County under USDA ReConnect funding rounds.

restrictions.name: Pembina County
restrictions.title: Pembina County ND — Cavalier/NE North Dakota/Canadian border/Pembina Trail history
restrictions.description: Pembina County in northeastern North Dakota was the site of the first permanent settlement in what is now North Dakota — the Pembina settlement dating to 1797, where fur traders established a Red River Valley post. The Pembina Trail was a major 19th-century trade route between Pembina and St. Paul, Minnesota. Cavalier is the county seat. The county borders Manitoba, Canada, and the Portal/Pembina border crossings are significant commercial truck crossings for US-Canada trade. CBP's commercial vehicle inspection systems and Canadian border IT infrastructure are significant federal computing investments in this remote agricultural county. Spring flooding on the Red River tributaries generates Army Corps water management IT.

restrictions.name: San Miguel County
restrictions.title: San Miguel County NM — Las Vegas NM/New Mexico Highlands University/Santa Fe Trail
restrictions.description: San Miguel County's county seat of Las Vegas, New Mexico (not to be confused with Las Vegas, Nevada) was a significant stop on the Santa Fe Trail and one of the largest cities in the Southwest in the 1880s. New Mexico Highlands University is a Hispanic-serving institution with a long history in this culturally rich city. Las Vegas NM's Victorian architecture and bilingual Spanish-English heritage make it one of New Mexico's most distinctive cultural landscapes. The county has been used as a film and TV production location (many westerns), generating production IT infrastructure. Storrie Lake State Park and the Gallinas River corridor provide water management infrastructure, and NMHU's technology programs serve as a workforce pipeline for the state's growing tech sector.

restrictions.name: Hidalgo County
restrictions.title: Hidalgo County NM — Lordsburg/extreme SW New Mexico/Arizona border/Chiricahua Apache history
restrictions.description: Hidalgo County occupies the extreme southwest corner of New Mexico — a remote, sparsely populated county bordering both Arizona and Mexico. Lordsburg is the county seat, on the Southern Pacific railroad line. The Peloncillo Mountains and Animas Valley here were the homeland of the Chiricahua Apache and the scene of Geronimo's final campaigns in the 1880s. Hidalgo County is one of the most remote counties in the contiguous United States for broadband access — it appears consistently in FCC coverage gap analyses as among the lowest-connected counties in New Mexico. The Whitewater Baldy fire (2012) and monitoring systems for the Gila National Forest contribute federal environmental IT to this 4,500-resident county.

restrictions.name: Freestone County
restrictions.title: Freestone County TX — Fairfield/Freestone County Power Project/lignite coal/east Texas
restrictions.description: Freestone County in east-central Texas contains the Big Brown Power Plant — once one of the largest lignite-fired power plants in the US, operated by Luminant (formerly TXU Energy). The plant closed in 2018, but its decades of operation created electrical transmission infrastructure scaled for a large generating facility. The decommissioning and brownfield redevelopment of the Big Brown site has attracted attention from developers interested in repurposing the existing grid connections. Fairfield is the county seat and a regional commercial center for a multi-county area. Freestone County's grid-scale transmission infrastructure and low land costs make it one of east Texas's more discussed potential data center development locations in ERCOT's deregulated power market.

restrictions.name: Lavaca County
restrictions.title: Lavaca County TX — Hallettsville/Czech heritage/Gulf Coast Prairies/Formosa adjacent
restrictions.description: Lavaca County in coastal-plain Texas is the heart of Texas's Czech immigrant settlement belt — Hallettsville, Shiner (in adjacent Lavaca County portions), and the surrounding communities were settled by Czech and German immigrants in the late 19th century. Shiner Brewery (Spoetzl Brewery), home of Shiner Bock, is located in Shiner within Lavaca County — one of the most iconic independent breweries in Texas. The county's position in the Gulf Coastal Plain between the San Antonio and Houston metropolitan areas gives it access to both markets' fiber backbone. Lavaca County's agricultural base (cattle, cotton, sorghum) and its position in the Eagle Ford Shale periphery creates energy sector IT interest.

restrictions.name: Prairie County
restrictions.title: Prairie County MT — Terry/Yellowstone River/badlands/eastern Montana oil/Fort Keogh adjacent
restrictions.description: Prairie County in eastern Montana centers on Terry — a small Yellowstone River town in Montana's oil-producing badlands. Fort Keogh (in adjacent Custer County at Miles City) was an important US Army post established after the Battle of Little Bighorn, and the Fort Keogh Livestock and Range Research Laboratory (USDA Agricultural Research Service) continues scientific work in the area. Prairie County's badlands terrain (similar to Theodore Roosevelt National Park in North Dakota) creates dramatic landscapes and occasional oil exploration. Eastern Montana's position in the USGS Northern Great Plains monitoring network contributes federal environmental IT. Montana-Dakota Utilities serves the county's electrical needs across Montana's isolated eastern plains.

restrictions.name: Toole County
restrictions.title: Toole County MT — Shelby/Sweetgrass/Canadian border/Oilmont/Glacier County adjacent
restrictions.description: Toole County in north-central Montana borders Alberta, Canada at the Sweetgrass-Coutts border crossing — one of the busiest commercial truck crossings on the US-Canada border. Shelby is the county seat, famous for hosting the Dempsey-Gibbons heavyweight boxing championship fight in 1923. The county has oil and gas production in the Sweetgrass Hills area and sits at the northern edge of Montana's oil-producing Williston Basin extension. The Sweetgrass-Coutts border crossing generates CBP commercial vehicle inspection IT and US-Canada trade logistics computing. The region's proximity to the Blackfeet Nation (adjacent Glacier County) adds tribal governance IT to the area's institutional computing profile.

restrictions.name: Jones County
restrictions.title: Jones County SD — Murdo/least populous SD county/I-90/Badlands adjacent
restrictions.description: Jones County is South Dakota's least populous county — with approximately 700 residents — and one of the most sparsely settled counties in the contiguous United States. Murdo, the county seat, sits on I-90 in the heart of the South Dakota plains, where the highway serves as the primary east-west logistics corridor across the state. The county is adjacent to Badlands National Park and the Buffalo Gap National Grassland. Jones County's extreme remoteness has made it a priority area for USDA ReConnect and FCC E-Rate rural broadband programs. Despite its tiny population, the county's I-90 position generates truck stop and logistics technology infrastructure.

restrictions.name: Lake County
restrictions.title: Lake County SD — Madison/Dakota State University/Lake Madison/DSU cyber research
restrictions.description: Lake County in eastern South Dakota is home to Dakota State University in Madison — one of the most focused cybersecurity and technology universities in the Upper Midwest. DSU operates the Madison Cyber Labs (MCL), a state-of-the-art cybersecurity research facility, and has strong partnerships with the US Department of Defense's Cyber Command. DSU's technology focus makes Lake County an unusual case: a rural South Dakota county with a nationally recognized cybersecurity research program. The Dakota Cyber Summit, hosted at DSU, draws government, military, and industry cybersecurity professionals. Lake Madison and Lake Herman are recreational lakes attracting second-home development that drives local broadband investment.

restrictions.name: Brule County
restrictions.title: Brule County SD — Chamberlain/Lake Francis Case/Missouri River/I-90 gateway
restrictions.description: Brule County sits where I-90 crosses the Missouri River at Chamberlain — one of South Dakota's most significant highway waypoints between the east and west halves of the state. Lake Francis Case, created by Fort Randall Dam (a US Army Corps of Engineers project on the Missouri River), provides water recreation and federal water management IT. Chamberlain is a traditional stopping point for travelers and serves the Crow Creek Sioux Reservation nearby. The Akta Lakota Museum at St. Joseph's Indian School in Chamberlain preserves Lakota heritage and generates educational IT. Missouri River hydroelectric management from the Pick-Sloan reservoirs creates significant USACE computing infrastructure at Chamberlain's dam-related facilities.

restrictions.name: Bureau County
restrictions.title: Bureau County IL — Princeton/Illinois and Michigan Canal/Heritage Corridor/I-80 rail hub
restrictions.description: Bureau County in north-central Illinois contains Princeton — a historic market town on the Illinois River. The Illinois and Michigan Canal, which connected Lake Michigan to the Illinois River and catalyzed Chicago's growth as a rail-canal hub, runs through Bureau County and is now a Heritage Corridor managed by the National Park Service. Bureau County is one of Illinois's more productive agricultural counties in the Illinois River valley. Spoon River (of Edgar Lee Masters' 'Spoon River Anthology' fame) flows through western Bureau County. The county's I-80 corridor position gives it access to major freight rail and highway infrastructure connecting Chicago to the West Coast.

restrictions.name: Crawford County
restrictions.title: Crawford County IL — Robinson/Lincoln Trail/Illinois oilfields/southeast Illinois
restrictions.description: Crawford County in southeast Illinois was the first commercial oil-producing county in Illinois — oil was discovered near Robinson in 1905, predating the more famous Illinois Basin discoveries. Robinson remains a petroleum service and refining center, with Marathon Oil's Robinson refinery being one of Illinois's primary petroleum refining facilities. The Lincoln Trail runs through Crawford County — connecting Vincennes, Indiana to Springfield, Lincoln's Prairie. The county's petroleum infrastructure (refinery IT, pipeline monitoring, terminal management) creates industrial computing in a rural county of 19,000 people. Olney (adjacent Richland County) adds to the southeast Illinois petroleum corridor.

restrictions.name: De Witt County
restrictions.title: De Witt County IL — Clinton/Clinton Lake/Illinois Power nuclear/central Illinois
restrictions.description: De Witt County in central Illinois contains Clinton Lake — a 4,895-acre cooling reservoir built for the Clinton Power Station, a nuclear generating station operated by Exelon. Clinton Power Station is a single-unit nuclear facility that provides baseload power to Illinois's grid. Nuclear power plant operations create significant safety-critical IT: reactor monitoring systems, NRC regulatory compliance, security systems, and maintenance management computing. The Sangamon River drainage and central Illinois agricultural production contribute to the county's character. De Witt County's position in Illinois's energy generating infrastructure makes it institutionally significant relative to its population of 15,000.

restrictions.name: Sublette County
restrictions.title: Sublette County WY — Pinedale/Jonah natural gas field/Wyoming Range/Green River Basin
restrictions.description: Sublette County in western Wyoming contains the Jonah Gas Field and Pinedale Anticline — one of the most productive natural gas fields in the Rocky Mountain region, producing at its peak billions of cubic feet of natural gas per day. Pinedale is the county seat, a small town that became a boomtown during the gas field development of the 2000s. The Green River Basin's natural gas infrastructure includes thousands of wells, gathering systems, and processing plants — all requiring sophisticated SCADA, well monitoring, and production data management. Sublette County's remote mountain terrain and extensive federal land management (BLM, Forest Service) creates additional government IT infrastructure.

restrictions.name: Hot Springs County
restrictions.title: Hot Springs County WY — Thermopolis/world's largest mineral hot spring/Big Horn Basin
restrictions.description: Hot Springs County contains Thermopolis — home to Hot Springs State Park, which contains what is claimed to be the world's largest mineral hot spring. The Thermopolis hot springs flow at over 8 million gallons per day. Wyoming Dinosaur Center in Thermopolis is a world-class dinosaur museum with active excavation sites in the surrounding Bighorn Basin. Wind River Canyon — a dramatic gorge cut through the Owl Creek Mountains by the Wind River — provides a geological spectacle and National Scenic Byway in Hot Springs County. The county's tourism and geological research infrastructure create unusual digital needs for a county of fewer than 5,000 people.

restrictions.name: Teton County
restrictions.title: Teton County WY — Jackson Hole/Grand Teton NP/Jackson Hole Economic Summit/billionaire resort
restrictions.description: Teton County has the highest median household income of any county in the United States in most years — and by some measures the highest per-capita income of any county in America — driven by Jackson Hole's concentration of ultra-wealthy seasonal and permanent residents. The Jackson Hole Economic Policy Symposium, hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City, convenes global central bankers annually — an event that moves global financial markets and requires extraordinary security and communications IT. Grand Teton National Park generates significant National Park Service digital infrastructure. Teton County's ultra-high-bandwidth demands from globally connected residents, financial summits, and luxury resort operations create a connectivity investment profile that far exceeds the county's 23,000 permanent residents.

restrictions.name: Benton County
restrictions.title: Benton County IN — Fowler/northwest Indiana wind energy/Benton County Wind Farm
restrictions.description: Benton County in northwest Indiana is one of the most wind-energy-dense counties in Indiana — home to multiple wind farm projects that make it a leader in Indiana's renewable energy transition. The flat, open agricultural landscape of the Kankakee River drainage provides excellent wind resources. Fowler is the county seat of this primarily agricultural county. Indiana Michigan Power (American Electric Power) and Duke Energy's transmission infrastructure crosses Benton County as part of the MISO grid management for northwest Indiana. The county's wind energy development has brought turbine monitoring SCADA and renewable energy grid management IT to an otherwise traditional corn and soybean farming county.

restrictions.name: Fayette County
restrictions.title: Fayette County IN — Connersville/Whitewater River/Inland manufacturing heritage
restrictions.description: Fayette County in eastern Indiana centers on Connersville — once known as 'Little Detroit' for its concentration of early automotive parts manufacturers. Inland Steel, Connersville Blower, and dozens of automotive components factories operated here in the early and mid 20th century. The Whitewater River valley provided power for early mills. Fayette County's deindustrialization (similar to many eastern Indiana counties) has left it seeking new economic identity, with broadband and digital infrastructure investments prioritized by the county's economic development programs. The Whitewater Valley Excursion Railroad (a scenic railway using restored steam locomotives) adds heritage tourism IT to the county's profile.

restrictions.name: Owen County
restrictions.title: Owen County IN — Spencer/Cataract Falls/White River/south-central Indiana
restrictions.description: Owen County in south-central Indiana is home to Cataract Falls State Recreation Area — the largest-volume waterfall in Indiana, on the Eel River (a White River tributary). Spencer is the county seat. The county's proximity to Bloomington (home of Indiana University, 20 miles east) positions it within IU's research network influence and increasingly within the Bloomington tech community's exurban commuter zone. Owen County's hardwood timber industry and limestone quarrying heritage connect it to Indiana's stone industry corridor. Hoosier National Forest's western boundary is nearby, and USFS recreation management IT extends toward Owen County's boundaries.

restrictions.name: Carter County
restrictions.title: Carter County MO — Van Buren/Current River/Ozark National Scenic Riverways/Ozark highlands
restrictions.description: Carter County in the Missouri Ozarks anchors the Current River corridor — the spring-fed river system that makes up the heart of the Ozark National Scenic Riverways (America's first national scenic riverway, established 1964). Van Buren is the county seat, on the Current River in one of the most remote sections of the Missouri Ozarks. Greer Spring — the largest spring in Missouri — is in Carter County, discharging an average of 222 million gallons per day into the Eleven Point River. The National Park Service's Ozark National Scenic Riverways management IT (visitor data, hydrological monitoring, permit systems) operates from this remote country. Carter County is among Missouri's most isolated counties from broadband access.

restrictions.name: Gasconade County
restrictions.title: Gasconade County MO — Hermann/Missouri River/German wine country/Meramec River
restrictions.description: Gasconade County is the heart of Missouri's German immigrant wine region — Hermann was founded in 1836 by German immigrants who deliberately sought a landscape resembling the Rhine Valley. Stone Hill Winery in Hermann is one of the largest wineries in the US by volume. The county sits where the Gasconade River meets the Missouri River — historically a significant confluence for river traffic. The Missouri Pacific Railroad's historic route through the Gasconade River valley was the scene of the deadliest railroad accident in Missouri history (1855 Gasconade River bridge collapse). Gasconade County's tourism economy and wine industry create hospitality and agritourism IT infrastructure.

restrictions.name: Iron County
restrictions.title: Iron County MO — Ironton/Taum Sauk Mountain/highest MO point/lead mining/SE Missouri
restrictions.description: Iron County in the Missouri Iron Mountain region contains Taum Sauk Mountain — the highest point in Missouri at 1,772 feet. Ironton is the county seat. The Taum Sauk Pumped Storage Project — an Ameren Missouri hydroelectric facility — operates on Proffit Mountain using two reservoirs and a 2,400-foot powerhead to generate up to 440 megawatts. In 2005, the upper reservoir suffered a catastrophic breach, releasing 1.3 billion gallons of water in about 12 minutes — a failure that reshaped dam safety monitoring requirements. The rebuilt facility's dam safety monitoring IT represents state-of-the-art infrastructure control systems. Iron County's lead mining heritage (Old Lead Belt) and current mining activity add industrial IT to this Ozarks county.

restrictions.name: Baker County
restrictions.title: Baker County GA — Newton/SW Georgia/Flint River/smallest GA county population
restrictions.description: Baker County is Georgia's least populous county — with fewer than 3,500 residents. Newton is the county seat, on the Flint River in southwest Georgia's rich agricultural flatwoods. Baker County's peanut, cotton, and timber production participates in Georgia's agricultural digital infrastructure, including University of Georgia Cooperative Extension precision agriculture programs. The Flint River here is monitored by USGS gauging stations as part of the Apalachicola-Chattahoochee-Flint (ACF) Basin water dispute monitoring — a multi-decade interstate water conflict between Georgia, Alabama, and Florida that requires extensive hydrological IT. Baker County's participation in Georgia's rural broadband priority mapping programs reflects its position as a digital equity challenge.

restrictions.name: Clinch County
restrictions.title: Clinch County GA — Homerville/Okefenokee Swamp gateway/SE Georgia timber
restrictions.description: Clinch County in southeast Georgia sits at the northern gateway to the Okefenokee Swamp National Wildlife Refuge — one of the largest blackwater swamp ecosystems in North America. Homerville is the county seat. The Okefenokee, managed by the US Fish and Wildlife Service, requires extensive ecological monitoring IT for water levels, wildlife tracking, fire management, and wilderness permit systems. Clinch County's timber industry (loblolly pine plantations) connects to the Georgia timber supply chain. The county's proximity to the I-75 corridor (a few miles east in Lowndes County) and the Jacksonville, FL metro's northern influence zone create commercial connectivity demand.

restrictions.name: Heard County
restrictions.title: Heard County GA — Franklin/Chattahoochee River/West Georgia/Troup County adjacent
restrictions.description: Heard County is a small, rural west Georgia county on the Chattahoochee River — bordering Alabama across the Chattahoochee. Franklin is the county seat. The county sits in the West Georgia development corridor where Troup County's Kia automotive plant (LaGrange) and the Columbus/Phenix City metro create economic spillover effects. Heard County's agricultural and timber base is complemented by its position in Georgia Power's renewable energy expansion territory. The Chattahoochee River here is part of the ACF Basin water management system, with USGS monitoring stations tracking flows relevant to the multi-state water compact. Georgia's rural broadband priority zone designations include Heard County.

restrictions.name: Dillingham Census Area
restrictions.title: Dillingham Census Area AK — Dillingham/Bristol Bay/largest sockeye salmon run/Pebble Mine controversy
restrictions.description: The Dillingham Census Area encompasses the Bristol Bay region of southwest Alaska — site of the world's largest sockeye salmon run, which supports a billion-dollar commercial fishing industry. Dillingham is the hub city for this remote area. The proposed Pebble Mine — a massive copper and gold deposit near the headwaters of rivers feeding Bristol Bay — has been one of the most contentious mining proposals in US history, with EPA and Army Corps regulatory proceedings generating extensive environmental IT and monitoring infrastructure. Bristol Bay Native Corporation and regional tribal governments maintain sophisticated fisheries management and cultural heritage IT. Satellite-dependent broadband (SpaceX Starlink has significantly improved connectivity) serves this remote region.

restrictions.name: Northwest Arctic Borough
restrictions.title: Northwest Arctic Borough AK — Kotzebue/Red Dog Mine/NANA Regional Corporation/Arctic telecommunications
restrictions.description: The Northwest Arctic Borough encompasses Kotzebue Sound and the Kobuk River drainage north of the Arctic Circle. Kotzebue is one of Alaska's largest hub communities above the Arctic Circle. The Red Dog Mine — operated by Teck Alaska under a partnership with NANA Regional Corporation — is one of the world's largest zinc mines and the economic engine of the region. Red Dog's mine operations require industrial SCADA, concentrate transport monitoring, and environmental compliance IT across one of the world's most remote mining operations. NANA Regional Corporation's network of 11 villages maintains sophisticated tribal administrative IT. GCI's Arctic fiber initiative and Starlink have brought improved connectivity to Kotzebue, making it a test case for Arctic broadband deployment.

restrictions.name: Casey County
restrictions.title: Casey County KY — Liberty/south-central Kentucky/ARC Appalachian edge
restrictions.description: Casey County in south-central Kentucky sits at the edge of the Appalachian Regional Commission's service territory — one of the transition counties between the Bluegrass agricultural region and the Appalachian coal counties to the east. Liberty is the county seat. The county's agricultural base includes tobacco, cattle, and row crops. Casey County has been a recipient of ARC broadband and infrastructure investment targeting the Appalachian transition zone. The county's position on the Cumberland Parkway corridor gives it improved highway access that has spurred some commercial development. Kentucky's rural broadband deployment programs have prioritized Casey County under the state's Connecting Kentucky initiative.

restrictions.name: Harlan County
restrictions.title: Harlan County KY — Harlan/Cumberland/coal wars legacy/Black Mountain/appalachian coal
restrictions.description: Harlan County, Kentucky is one of America's most historically significant labor battlegrounds — the 'Bloody Harlan' coal wars of the 1930s, documented by Theodore Dreiser and later in Barbara Kopple's Oscar-winning documentary 'Harlan County USA' (1976), defined the American labor movement's Appalachian chapter. Black Mountain — the highest point in Kentucky at 4,145 feet — is in Harlan County. The county's coal production peaked in the mid-20th century and has substantially declined, but significant mine safety IT (continuous monitoring systems mandated by MSHA) remains operational at active mines. Harlan County's dramatic terrain, labor history, and active ARC broadband investments make it institutionally important in Appalachian Kentucky.

restrictions.name: Cass County
restrictions.title: Cass County MN — Walker/Leech Lake Band Ojibwe/largest MN county/Paul Bunyan country
restrictions.description: Cass County is Minnesota's largest county by area and one of the most lake-dense counties in the United States — containing Leech Lake (one of Minnesota's largest lakes) and hundreds of smaller lakes. Walker is the county seat on Leech Lake. The Leech Lake Band of Ojibwe has sovereignty over reservation territory within Cass County and operates sophisticated tribal governance, natural resource management, and gaming IT (Northern Lights Casino). Chippewa National Forest covers much of Cass County, with US Forest Service management computing including carbon sequestration monitoring (Chippewa has large old-growth forest tracts). The Paul Bunyan Trail and recreational economy create resort and tourism IT.

restrictions.name: Lake of the Woods County
restrictions.title: Lake of the Woods County MN — Baudette/Northwest Angle/northernmost contiguous US point
restrictions.description: Lake of the Woods County contains the Northwest Angle — the northernmost point of the contiguous United States, accessible by land only through Canada. The anomaly resulted from a 1783 treaty boundary error. The county borders Ontario and Manitoba. Baudette is the county seat, on the Rainy River. Lake of the Woods itself (straddling the Minnesota-Ontario border) is one of the best walleye fishing lakes in the world, generating significant fishing tourism IT. The county's extreme remoteness and international border position require CBP processing systems for the border crossings from Canada, even for American citizens traveling to the Northwest Angle.

restrictions.name: McLeod County
restrictions.title: McLeod County MN — Glencoe/Hutchinson/3M printing heritage/Minnesota River Valley
restrictions.description: McLeod County in south-central Minnesota is home to Hutchinson — a manufacturing and healthcare hub in the Minnesota River Valley. 3M's printing and imaging manufacturing legacy (including the former Hutchinson Technology hard disk drive operations) created a significant industrial IT heritage. Hutchinson Technology was for decades one of the world's leading manufacturers of suspension assemblies for hard disk drives — an export-oriented precision manufacturing industry in a small Minnesota city. The county's Minnesota River Valley agriculture and its manufacturing heritage create a distinctive combination of precision industrial and agricultural IT. Ridgewater College's Hutchinson campus provides technical education for the region.

restrictions.name: Montague County
restrictions.title: Montague County TX — Bowie/Red River/north Texas oil/Chisholm Trail heritage
restrictions.description: Montague County in north Texas borders the Red River and Oklahoma, in the heart of the North Texas oil producing region. Bowie is the county seat — named for James Bowie of the Alamo. The Chisholm Trail historically passed through this region, connecting south Texas cattle ranches to Kansas railheads. Montague County has oil and gas production connected to the Barnett Shale's northern extension. The Red River's meandering channel (historically disputed between Texas and Oklahoma in the Kiowa-Comanche Treaty land dispute) is monitored by USGS gauging stations. The county's position near Wichita Falls (30 miles south) gives it access to the mid-Texas telecom infrastructure.

restrictions.name: Clay County
restrictions.title: Clay County TX — Henrietta/north Texas Red River/Lake Arrowhead/oil and gas
restrictions.description: Clay County in north Texas centers on Henrietta, a small county seat near Lake Arrowhead — a water supply reservoir for Wichita Falls that also provides recreation. The county has oil and gas production in the North Texas basin. Clay County's position between Wichita Falls (the metro anchor of north Texas) and the Oklahoma border creates a corridor for agricultural and energy sector IT. Lake Arrowhead is managed by the City of Wichita Falls water utility, with water management and treatment IT serving the larger metropolitan area. The county's ranching and wheat farming landscape is characteristic of the Rolling Plains of North Texas.

restrictions.name: Dixon County
restrictions.title: Dixon County NE — Ponca/NE Nebraska/Ponca State Park/Missouri River bluffs
restrictions.description: Dixon County in northeast Nebraska borders the Missouri River along one of its most scenic stretches — the river bluffs of the Ponca region that impressed Lewis and Clark on their 1804 passage. Ponca State Park on the Missouri River bluffs is a significant Nebraska state park. Dixon County sits in the heartland of the Iowa/Nebraska border agricultural corridor, where grain farming and hog production create agricultural supply chain IT. The Ponca Tribe of Nebraska's reservation and tribal government are centered near Niobrara (adjacent Knox County), and tribal programs extend into Dixon County's communities. NebraskaLand National Bank and FCS (Farm Credit Services) agricultural lending IT serves the county's farming operations.

restrictions.name: Custer County
restrictions.title: Custer County NE — Broken Bow/Nebraska Sandhills edge/Middle Loup River/largest NE county area
restrictions.description: Custer County is Nebraska's largest county by area — a vast central Nebraska county at the edge of the Nebraska Sandhills, where the transition from irrigated cropland to grass-covered dunes begins. Broken Bow is the county seat. The Middle Loup River (one of the Loup River system's main branches) originates in the Sandhills and flows through Custer County. The county's agricultural water management — particularly the Central Nebraska Public Power and Irrigation District's operations in the Loup River basin — requires sophisticated water management IT. Broken Bow's wind energy corridor position (Nebraska's central corridor has excellent wind resources) has attracted transmission infrastructure investment.

restrictions.name: Iowa County
restrictions.title: Iowa County IA — Marengo/Iowa River/Amana Colonies/east-central Iowa
restrictions.description: Iowa County in east-central Iowa is home to the Amana Colonies — a National Historic Landmark district consisting of seven villages founded in 1855 by German pietist immigrants who established a communal religious society. The Colonies maintained communal ownership until 1932 (when they incorporated as a for-profit corporation) and remain one of the longest-lasting communal societies in American history. Amana Appliances (formerly Amana Refrigeration, founded by the Colonies) became a major appliance manufacturer — now owned by Whirlpool — that maintains its manufacturing connection to the region. The University of Iowa's influence extends into Iowa County (30 miles from Iowa City), creating connectivity expectations for a county that hosts significant heritage tourism.

restrictions.name: Keokuk County
restrictions.title: Keokuk County IA — Sigourney/south-central Iowa/English River corridor
restrictions.description: Keokuk County in south-central Iowa centers on Sigourney, a small agricultural market town on the English River. The county's agriculture is typical of Iowa's transitional zone between the more productive northern Iowa row crop belt and the rougher south-central terrain. Iowa's rural broadband deployment programs, funded by USDA ReConnect and state digital equity programs, have prioritized south-central Iowa counties like Keokuk where connectivity gaps are most significant. The county's agricultural operations include cattle, hog, and row crop production with Alliant Energy's power distribution serving rural customers across the English River watershed.

restrictions.name: Mitchell County
restrictions.title: Mitchell County IA — Osage/Cedar River/north Iowa/Upper Iowa River adjacent
restrictions.description: Mitchell County in north Iowa centers on Osage — a small agricultural market town on the Cedar River headwaters. The county is adjacent to the Iowa-Minnesota border in a region where the Upper Iowa River system creates some of Iowa's most distinctive topography (the Driftless Area influences reach into this region). Mitchell County's agricultural base includes dairy, hog, and row crop operations. ITC Midwest's high-voltage transmission infrastructure crosses north Iowa counties including Mitchell as part of the wind energy build-out connecting northwest Iowa's wind farms to Midwestern load centers. North Iowa Area Community College's Osage learning center provides technical education locally.

restrictions.name: Hancock County
restrictions.title: Hancock County TN — Sneedville/Virginia border/Melungeon heritage/Powell River
restrictions.description: Hancock County in far northeast Tennessee is one of the most isolated counties in the state — a remote Powell Valley county on the Virginia border. Sneedville is the county seat. The county is historically associated with the Melungeon people — a tri-racial group of mixed European, African, and Native American ancestry whose documented history in the isolated Appalachian mountains of Hancock County dates to the early 19th century. Hancock County's extreme isolation has made it a persistent broadband challenge area in Tennessee's digital equity programs. The Powell River valley's agricultural and coal heritage creates an Appalachian identity that differs from Tennessee's more-connected central counties.

restrictions.name: Overton County
restrictions.title: Overton County TN — Livingston/upper Cumberland Plateau/Cordell Hull Lake
restrictions.description: Overton County in north-central Tennessee occupies part of the upper Cumberland Plateau — a region of caves, sinkholes, and dissected plateau terrain between the Nashville Basin and the Tennessee Valley. Livingston is the county seat. Cordell Hull Lake (impounded by the Army Corps of Engineers on the Cumberland River upstream from Cordell Hull Dam) provides flood control and recreation infrastructure. The lake is named for Cordell Hull — FDR's Secretary of State and the longest-serving Secretary of State in US history, born in Overton County in 1871. Tennessee Tech University is in adjacent Putnam County, and its influence extends into Overton County's workforce development programs.

restrictions.name: Stone County
restrictions.title: Stone County MS — Wiggins/SE Mississippi/De Soto National Forest/pine belt
restrictions.description: Stone County in southeast Mississippi is a pine belt county adjacent to the De Soto National Forest — one of Mississippi's largest national forests. Wiggins is the county seat, in the center of Mississippi's longleaf pine timber region. The county's timber industry creates forestry management IT and wood product supply chain computing. The Mississippi Gulf Coast's northward economic influence (Gulfport is 40 miles south) creates broadband and logistics infrastructure investment. Stone County's position in Mississippi's rural broadband priority zones under the state's Connect Mississippi initiative reflects its persistent connectivity challenges despite its relatively close proximity to the Gulf Coast metro.

restrictions.name: Franklin County
restrictions.title: Franklin County MS — Meadville/Homochitto National Forest/SW Mississippi/oil legacy
restrictions.description: Franklin County in southwest Mississippi contains Meadville — a small county seat surrounded by the Homochitto National Forest. The Homochitto NF is one of Mississippi's largest national forests, managed by the US Forest Service with timber management, recreation, and ecological monitoring IT. Franklin County has oil and gas production in the Southwest Mississippi oil field complex that includes the Tinsley and Baxterville fields. The county's position in the Natchez Trace corridor (the Natchez Trace Parkway passes near the county) connects it to NPS management IT. Mississippi Delta Development Authority programs extend into southwest Mississippi counties including Franklin for rural development investment.

restrictions.name: Clearwater County
restrictions.title: Clearwater County ID — Orofino/Nez Perce National Forest/Dworshak Dam/Lewis and Clark trail
restrictions.description: Clearwater County in north-central Idaho centers on Orofino on the Clearwater River — a major tributary of the Snake River that Lewis and Clark descended in 1805 after crossing the Bitterroot Mountains. Dworshak Dam at Orofino is one of the highest straight-axis gravity dams in the Western Hemisphere — and created the largest federal fish hatchery complex in the US to mitigate steelhead and chinook salmon impacts. The Army Corps of Engineers' Dworshak operations include dam safety monitoring, fish hatchery management IT, and reservoir management computing. Nez Perce National Forest provides USFS management IT across millions of acres. Clearwater County's timber industry (the Clearwater River watershed is major timber country) adds forest products IT.

restrictions.name: Benewah County
restrictions.title: Benewah County ID — St. Maries/Coeur d'Alene Lake South Fork/Coeur d'Alene Tribe
restrictions.description: Benewah County in northern Idaho's Panhandle sits south of Coeur d'Alene Lake on the St. Maries River. St. Maries is the county seat. The Coeur d'Alene Tribe's reservation territory includes Benewah County, and the tribe's Coeur d'Alene Casino Resort (in adjacent Kootenai County near Worley) generates significant gaming and tribal enterprise IT. The Coeur d'Alene Tribe is notable for its successful effort to have Lake Coeur d'Alene recognized as tribal trust land in a 2001 US Supreme Court decision. The St. Joe National Forest and the St. Joe River (one of the highest navigable rivers in the US) create USFS management and recreation IT in the county.

restrictions.name: Adams County
restrictions.title: Adams County OH — West Union/Serpent Mound/Edge of Appalachia/southern Ohio
restrictions.description: Adams County in southern Ohio contains Great Serpent Mound — the world's largest surviving prehistoric effigy mound, built by the ancient Fort Ancient culture (or possibly the earlier Adena culture) in the shape of a serpent over 1,300 feet long. The mound is a National Historic Landmark and Ohio Historical Society site, with preservation and visitor management IT. Adams County is also home to Edge of Appalachia Preserve — a Nature Conservancy and Cincinnati Museum Center partnership preserving one of Ohio's most biodiverse landscapes, where the Appalachian plateau meets the Bluegrass Region. The county's remoteness (one of Ohio's least populous and least connected) makes it a persistent broadband priority for Ohio's connectivity programs.
