The Utica Shale formation runs through Harrison County, contributing to eastern Ohio's natural gas production. Harrison County's Appalachian character creates rural broadband and economic development challenges.

restrictions.name: Paulding County
restrictions.title: Paulding County OH — Paulding/northwest Ohio/Maumee River/Black Swamp drainage/flat tile-drained
restrictions.description: Paulding County in northwest Ohio is part of the historic Black Swamp region — one of the most extensive wetland areas in North America before 19th-century drainage. The Black Swamp (covering most of northwest Ohio and northeast Indiana) was drained by an extraordinary public works program of clay tile drainage in the 1860s-1880s, transforming swamp into some of the most productive corn and soybean farmland in the world. Paulding is the county seat. The Maumee River flows through Paulding County toward Toledo. Northwest Ohio's agricultural productivity — enabled by the Black Swamp drainage — is among the highest in the US, and the county's nutrient management IT (addressing Maumee River phosphorus loading that contributes to Lake Erie algal blooms) is a significant environmental management priority.

restrictions.name: Claiborne County
restrictions.title: Claiborne County MS — Port Gibson/Grand Gulf Military Park/Port Hudson adj/MS River/nuclear
restrictions.description: Claiborne County in western Mississippi sits on the Mississippi River — Port Gibson is the county seat, described by Ulysses S. Grant as 'too beautiful to burn' during the Vicksburg Campaign (1863). The Grand Gulf Military Monument preserves the site of the Battle of Grand Gulf (1863) — a Confederate fort on the Mississippi that Grant bypassed in his flanking movement on Vicksburg. The Grand Gulf Nuclear Station (Mississippi Power & Light) is located in Claiborne County — one of Mississippi's two nuclear power plants, providing baseload electricity and requiring significant radiation monitoring and safety IT. Claiborne County is one of Mississippi's most economically distressed counties despite the nuclear plant employment.

restrictions.name: Clarke County
restrictions.title: Clarke County MS — Quitman/SE Mississippi/Chickasawhay River/timber/Dunn's Falls/rural
restrictions.description: Clarke County in southeastern Mississippi centers on Quitman on the Chickasawhay River. Dunn's Falls Water Park in Clarke County preserves a historic 1857 gristmill on an 65-foot waterfall — one of Mississippi's most unusual geological features in an otherwise flat state. The county's longleaf pine timber economy connects to the Chickasawhay River timber industry that historically floated logs to Mobile Bay mills. Clarke County's agricultural economy (soybeans, cattle, timber) and its position in the Mississippi timber belt create forestry management IT. The county is part of the larger Hattiesburg/Laurel trade area. Mississippi's rural broadband initiative includes Clarke County as a priority deployment area.

restrictions.name: Copiah County
restrictions.title: Copiah County MS — Hazlehurst/south-central Mississippi/Natchez Trace/I-55 corridor
restrictions.description: Copiah County in south-central Mississippi centers on Hazlehurst on I-55 and the historic Illinois Central Railroad line. The Natchez Trace Parkway passes through Copiah County — the 444-mile NPS unit following the ancient trace from Nashville to Natchez through the Mississippi heartland. Hazlehurst is the birthplace of Robert Johnson, the Delta blues musician whose 1936-37 recordings (including 'Cross Road Blues') are foundational to American popular music — Johnson's mysterious early death at 27 and the legend of his 'deal with the devil' at a Mississippi crossroads have made him one of American music's most mythologized figures. Copiah County's agriculture (soybeans, cotton, timber) and I-55 corridor logistics create infrastructure IT.

restrictions.name: Pendleton County
restrictions.title: Pendleton County WV — Franklin/Spruce Knob/Seneca Rocks/Monongahela NF/highest WV point
restrictions.description: Pendleton County in eastern West Virginia is home to Spruce Knob — West Virginia's highest point (4,863 feet) and the center of the Spruce Knob-Seneca Rocks National Recreation Area. Seneca Rocks, a dramatic quartzite fin rising 900 feet above the North Fork Valley, is one of the most challenging and celebrated rock climbing destinations in the eastern US. Franklin is the county seat of one of West Virginia's most rural and picturesque counties. The Monongahela National Forest covers most of Pendleton County. The county's Helvetia community — settled by Swiss German immigrants in 1869 and maintaining Swiss cultural traditions including Fasnacht festival — is one of the most intact 19th-century ethnic enclaves in West Virginia.

restrictions.name: Tucker County
restrictions.title: Tucker County WV — Parsons/Blackwater Falls/Dolly Sods Wilderness/Monongahela NF/ski
restrictions.description: Tucker County in northeastern West Virginia centers on Parsons in the Cheat River drainage. Blackwater Falls State Park — with its spectacular amber-colored falls (tinted by tannic acid from fallen hemlock and red spruce needles) — is one of West Virginia's most photographed locations. The Dolly Sods Wilderness Area in the Monongahela National Forest preserves a high-altitude plateau landscape of wind-sculpted red spruce, heath barrens, and boulder fields that feels more like Labrador than the mid-Atlantic — the result of 19th-century clearcutting and repeated wildfires that created an unusual sub-alpine succession. Canaan Valley Resort (Tucker County) is West Virginia's largest ski resort. Tucker County's Highlands region draws outdoor recreation from the DC metro area.
